title: Dirty NIF registration API and scheduling wrapper
purpose: B-077 created the dirty thread pools and B-078 wired the interpreter to use them. This brief provides the public API for registering NIFs as dirty and adds the wrapper that host programs (beamr-cli, beamr-meridian) use to declare dirty NIFs. It also identifies the existing BIFs that should be marked dirty (file I/O, shell commands, timer:sleep) and re-registers them.
task: Add a `register_dirty` API to the BIF registry, create a scheduling wrapper type for ergonomic dirty NIF declaration, and mark existing I/O-bound BIFs as dirty.

requirements.title: register_dirty API on BifRegistryImpl
requirements.spec: Add a registration variant that accepts a `DirtySchedulerKind` parameter.
acceptance: `BifRegistryImpl::register_dirty(module, function, arity, native_fn, kind: DirtySchedulerKind)` registers a NIF with dirty scheduling
Import resolution returns the correct dirty_kind when looking up the NIF
Test: register a dirty NIF, look it up, verify dirty_kind is Some(Cpu)

requirements.title: Re-register I/O-bound BIFs as dirty
requirements.spec: Identify existing BIFs that perform blocking I/O and re-register them as dirty IO. These include file operations, shell command execution, and sleep.
acceptance: `timer:sleep/1` → dirty IO (or CPU — debatable, but IO matches BEAM convention)
All re-registered BIFs continue to produce correct results
Test: calling read_file executes on a dirty IO thread (verify via thread name or pool metrics)

requirements.title: DirtyNif wrapper type for host programs
requirements.spec: Provide an ergonomic wrapper that host programs use to declare dirty NIFs without manually constructing NativeEntry.
acceptance: `DirtyNif::cpu(function: NativeFn) -> NativeEntry` creates a CPU-dirty entry
`DirtyNif::io(function: NativeFn) -> NativeEntry` creates an IO-dirty entry
Host programs (beamr-meridian) can use this API when registering custom NIFs
Test: DirtyNif::cpu produces NativeEntry with dirty_kind=Some(Cpu)
Test: DirtyNif::io produces NativeEntry with dirty_kind=Some(Io)

boundaries: SHALL NOT change existing NIF function signatures — dirty is a scheduling annotation, not a code change
SHALL NOT make all NIFs dirty by default — only explicitly marked NIFs run on dirty threads
SHALL NOT implement dirty NIF cancellation or timeout — dirty calls run to completion
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
